title: Performance, Migration, API Design, and Release Evidence · Guided Contract Studio
summary: Migrate and publish a production-shaped TypeScript 7 library with measurable compiler performance and consumer proof. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a TypeScript 7 migration and release portfolio while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.
Migrate JavaScript incrementally with checkJs, JSDoc, allowJs, boundary validation, and shrinking suppression budgets.
Remove TypeScript 6 deprecations, compare TS6 and TS7 diagnostics, and document temporary API-tool compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 5.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for measure cold and warm checks, project graphs, declarations, memory, and parallelism before changing configuration.

content.text: A workshop build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 5.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Announcing TypeScript 7.0 — Controls native compiler behavior, parallelism, TypeScript 7 defaults, removed options, JS changes, and TS6 API compatibility.
